title: [Snorlax] Battle Guide
summary: A 160 HP [Normal]-type special [wall] with [thick-fat] that halves [Ice] and [Fire] damage, combining [tank] and breaker duties in one slot.
strengths: 160 HP plus 110 Special Defense is among the best special bulk in the tier
110 Attack still hits hard enough to break [wall]s directly
weaknesses: 65 Defense is low for the body's size — physical breakers hurt
30 Speed moves last against virtually everything
[Fighting] weakness is exploited by common priority attackers

recommendedMoves.reason: [Normal] STAB with a 30 percent paralysis chance

recommendedItems.reason: Passive [recover]y stacks with the enormous HP stat

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Snorlax] is a [Normal]-type special [wall] whose 160 HP stat makes it one of the most durable Pokemon. With the [thick-fat] ability active, its two most common special weaknesses. Unlike most walls, [Snorlax] carries a meaningful offensive threat at 110 Attack, so it can function as a hybrid [tank] that both blocks threats and pressures them back with [body-slam] or [earthquake] on follow-up turns.

## Strengths
the 160/110 HP plus Special Defense line makes [Snorlax] the best special [wall] of its era, and [thick-fat] pushes that line even further by halving [Ice] and [Fire] damage. The 110 Attack means [Snorlax] isn't passive. The move pool also supports a [rest] plus [sleep-talk] build, a [curse] setup sweeper build, or a simple four-attack coverage pivot, making [Snorlax] extremely flexible depending on team needs.

## Weaknesses & Answers
The 65 Defense stat is the structural problem. Any strong physical breaker. [Ghost] walls also stop the [Normal] STAB completely, forcing [Snorlax] to commit to coverage moves. At 30 Speed, [Snorlax] moves last in every meaningful situation, so priority moves and status effects compound the defensive hole.

## Recommended Moveset
[body-slam] is the locked [Normal] STAB because the paralysis chance is actively useful as a Speed-control tool. [earthquake] handles [Steel], [Electric], and [Poison] switch-ins that would otherwise [absorb] [body-slam]. [crunch] covers [Ghost] and [Psychic] walls that blank both other attacks. The fourth slot is [rest] for sustained long-game plans; alternative builds swap it for [curse] to turn [Snorlax] into a slow setup sweeper, or [sleep-talk] for the classic [rest] talk build.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[leftovers] is the default. [chesto-berry] pairs with [rest] to get a one-turn full heal without the sleep downtime. The ability must be [thick-fat]. Tera [Fairy] flips the [Fighting] weakness into a resist and adds [Dragon] and [Dark] resistances on top, while Tera [Normal] maxes [body-slam] damage.

## Teammates & Synergy
A [Fighting] and [Ghost] answer is required. A physical [wall] that covers the low Defense stat (any bulky [Steel]) creates a defensive core. A fast cleaner on the back line converts the tempo [Snorlax] buys into wins, and a status absorber helps prevent [toxic] stall from wearing [Snorlax] down before [rest] can trigger.

## Play Patterns
Bring [Snorlax] in on predictable special attacks and [absorb] them passively. Attack with the move that matches the incoming switch. Use [rest] when HP drops below 60 percent, since [chesto-berry] only triggers once. For the [curse] build, set up on predicted switches and keep building boosts until [Snorlax] can't be [revenge]-killed. Save the Tera for the moment a [Fighting] move is confirmed.
